lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The staff directory is three years out of date. Reza knows this because the lamination is lifting at the corner and because Pemberton's name is still on it, and Pemberton retired the spring Reza started his second year.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He finds a newer one in the second drawer — the one kept for locum orientation packets, updated quarterly, photocopied on cardstock that smells faintly of the old copier on the administrative floor.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He sets the chart beside it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The handwriting on the entry is deliberate. Not hurried. Someone who writes like this writes like this always — the same slope, the same break between letters, a slight lift before a capital. A habit, not a decision.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Twelve attending physicians currently credentialed. Seven nurses with chart-entry clearance from that period. Two residents whose rotations would have overlapped.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He is not looking for a match. He is eliminating everything that isn't one.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The name arrives without announcement. It is simply there when he reaches it — third from the bottom of the administrative board roster, added as a footnote to the attending list because his clinical role ended years ago and they ran out of room on the main page.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Dr. Callum Farr. Board member. Former attending.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The attending who supervised Reza's intern year.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza puts his finger on the name and leaves it there. He reads it again. He reads the chart entry again. He does this three times, not because he doubts the match but because the action of reading takes up space that something else is trying to occupy.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He says it aloud to no one. The length of Farr's career. The armor of it. The number of residents who passed through his hands, which means the number of people who owe him something, or fear him something, which at this level of the institution amounts to the same thing.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The break room hum resumes its frequency.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza closes the directory. He does not close the chart.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The entry sits open on the table — one paragraph, one date, one name implied by every stroke of it — and Reza sits across from it the way you sit across from a person you have finally understood is not who you thought they were.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Careful. Still. Deciding what that means for every conversation that came before.
